preamble: The shift from an empty position to the voiced dental fricative [ð] is a specialized form of epenthesis. It typically occurs to break a hiatus between vowels or as a result of the intervocalic lenition of an underlying dental stop.

examples.note: In some Spanish varieties, an intrusive [ð] appears between vowel-final and vowel-initial words to resolve hiatus. NOTE: the primary Spanish example 'nada→naða' is d→ð (spirantization), not ∅→ð.

sources: Campbell, L. (2013). Historical Linguistics: An Introduction. MIT Press.
